name: Cowell Gymnasium and Outdoor Courts
description: Three courts indoors $5 per session per person. One court painted so play can occur over a lowered tennis net. Two courts painted for portable nets. Three courts marked off for chalking.  Shelburne Recreation runs regularly scheduled play sessions both indoors and outdoors. Recreation provides nets, paddles and balls for these sponsored sessions. Outdoor sessions are free. Signup required for indoor play. No signup required for outdoor play (suggested).  See Shelburne Falls Pickleball on Facebook for more information. https://www.facebook.com/groups/964695634932927
open_play_schedule: Sun, Tues, Thurs., Sat mornings. Mon., and Wed. evenings

name: Deerfield Railyards Pickleball
description: We are part of a multisport facility including simulator Golf.  Nice indoor space, good lighting, restrooms,  just  off the I 91 Greenfield rotary and not far from Treehouse brewery.  Check us out on playby point and the livestream camera for up to date  action.
fees: Fee required — $10 drop ins, play for daily play,  Memberships starting at $79

name: Greenfield YMCA
description: Greenfield YMCA is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Greenfield, MA. There are 3 indoor courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. You can reserve the courts. A membership is required to play.

name: Green River Park
description:
3 taped courts with portable nets plus one fenced in permanent pickleball court with painted lines and a permanent net.

Community here plays year round! 

Connect with local players at: https://www.facebook.com/groups/655684404790747

name: Hawlemont Elementary School
description: Hawlemont Elementary School is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Charlemont, MA. There is 1 outdoor hard court. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The court is free.
